title: BYOD Onboarding Tracking
dataSources: ISE BYOD portal logs, certificate provisioning
app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, HPE Aruba ClearPass syslog, Forescout CounterACT syslog
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:byod"
| stats count by device_type, os_type, onboarding_status
| sort -count
description: BYOD onboarding metrics inform mobile device management strategy and user experience optimization.
value: IT teams track the BYOD onboarding pipeline — from attempted registrations to completed enrollment and MDM compliance — identifying OS-specific failures and devices that register but never achieve compliance.
implementation: Track BYOD registrations, device types, and onboarding success/failure rates. Alert on onboarding failures. Report on device type distribution for MDM policy planning.
visualization: Pie chart (device types), Bar chart (onboarding status), Line chart (BYOD enrollment trend).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco ISE BYOD Portal with Dual-SSID or Single-SSID onboarding. Data in `index=nac` with `sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog`. Key fields: `UserName`, `CallingStationID`, `EndpointOUI`, `OperatingSystem`, `BYODRegistration` (Success/Failure), `MDMComplianceStatus`, `CertificateGeneratedDate`.
• ISE My Devices Portal allows users to register personal devices. ISE can generate supplicant profiles that install certificates and configure 802.1X automatically.
• MDM integration (e.g., Microsoft Intune, Workspace ONE): ISE queries MDM for compliance status of BYOD devices.
• Create a `byod_device_types.csv` lookup: `oui_prefix,manufacturer,device_category` to classify devices by OUI.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify BYOD events:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" earliest=-7d
| where isnotnull(BYODRegistration)
| stats count by BYODRegistration, OperatingSystem
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — BYOD onboarding funnel:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" earliest=-30d
| eval onboard_stage=case(
    BYODRegistration="Success", "3_Registered",
    BYODRegistration="Failure", "2_Failed",
    isnotnull(BYODRegistration), "1_Attempted",
    1==1, "0_Unknown"
  )
| stats count by onboard_stage, OperatingSystem
| sort onboard_stage
```

Understanding this SPL: Tracks the onboarding funnel — how many users attempt, how many fail, and how many complete registration. A high failure rate on a specific OS (especially iOS or Android) often means the supplicant profile is not compatible with the latest OS version.

MDM compliance check:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" earliest=-7d
| where BYODRegistration="Success"
| stats count by MDMComplianceStatus, OperatingSystem
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) In ISE: Work Centers > BYOD > Reports.
(b) Register a test BYOD device and verify the event.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Onboarding funnel. Row 2 — Failures by OS. Row 3 — MDM compliance.
Report: Weekly BYOD onboarding summary to IT management.

Runbook:
1. **iOS failures spike** — Check Apple's recent OS update for EAP-TLS changes.
2. **MDM non-compliant BYOD** — Device does not meet security policy. IT can remediate or revoke access.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No BYOD events** — BYOD Portal may not be enabled in ISE. Check Work Centers > BYOD.
• **`BYODRegistration` field missing** — Requires detailed syslog logging level in ISE.
grandmaExplanation: We track how employees bring their personal phones and laptops onto our network — we can see who tried to connect, who succeeded, who failed, and whether each personal device meets our security requirements.
hardware: Cisco ISE 3515/3595/3615/3655/3695, ISE Virtual Appliance; HPE Aruba ClearPass C1000/C2000/C3000, ClearPass Virtual Appliance; Forescout CounterACT CT-xxxx, eyeExtend
